title: Senior Sales Engineer
description_clean: As an Enterprise Sales Engineer, you will provide technical expertise through sales presentations, product demonstrations, and supporting technical evaluations (POVs). Sales Engineers help qualify and close opportunities with customers and partners and have a voice with the product team to help prioritize features based on input from customers, competitors, and partners. What You’ll Do: Partner with the Sales team to articulate the overall Datadog value proposition, vision and strategy to customers Own technical engagement with customers during the trial phase. Communicate Datadog’s value based on activities and work with customers on any identified issues or concerns to successful conclusion Technically close complex opportunities through advanced competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility Deliver product and technical briefings / presentations to potential clients Maintain accurate notes and feedback in CRM regarding customer input both wins and losses Proactively engage and communicate with customers and Datadog business/technical teams regarding product feedback and competitive landscape Who You Are: Passionate about educating customers on observability risks that are meaningful to their business, and able to build and execute an evaluation plan with a customer Someone with strong written and oral communication skills. This role requires an ability to understand and articulate both the business benefits (value proposition) and technical advantages of our offering Experienced in programming/scripting with any of the following: Java, Python, Ruby, Go, Node.JS, PHP, and .NET etc. Someone with a minimum of 3+ years in a Sales Engineering or DevOps Engineering role Able to sit up to 4 hours, traveling to and from client sites  Able to travel via auto, train or air up to 45% of the time Datadog values people from all walks of life. title: Product Manager II - Custom Metrics
description_clean: At Datadog, Custom Metrics sits at the heart of how our most sophisticated customers understand, govern, and scale their observability data. As we look toward 2026 and beyond, this area is becoming even more strategic — powering predictable growth, AI-driven insights, and new expansion opportunities across the Datadog platform. We’re looking for a Product Manager II to help own and deliver the next phase of Custom Metrics. This is a high-impact role operating at the intersection of infrastructure, storage, pricing, governance, and AI. You’ll take on technically deep problems, lead cross-product initiatives, and directly influence retention, expansion, and monetization for one of Datadog’s largest and fastest-evolving product areas. This role was created to close a critical execution gap and ensure we can deliver our 2026 roadmap with speed and confidence . At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them. What You’ll Do:  Own major initiatives on the Custom Metrics roadmap, with a focus on AI-driven governance, agentic workflows, and cross-product expansion into Infrastructure, Logs, and APM. Conduct regular customer conversations, analyze usage data and support feedback, and synthesize insights to inform product direction. Drive end-to-end execution for features and improvements — from concept and validation through launch and iteration. Define and track success metrics tied to adoption, usage, retention, and customer satisfaction. Partner closely with Engineering and Design to scope solutions, evaluate trade-offs, and deliver iterative value. Collaborate with adjacent product teams (e.g., Infrastructure, Logs, APM) to ensure alignment and smooth integrations where roadmaps intersect. Co-lead feature launches with Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success, ensuring clear positioning and enablement. Use data and customer feedback post-launch to refine features and improve impact. Support customer conversations, including handling escalations related to your product area and gathering direct feedback to inform roadmap decisions. Who You Are:  3 to 5 years of product management experience, preferably in technical B2B SaaS or platform products. Proven ownership of a product area or major feature set, driving initiatives from discovery through delivery and iteration. Strong product discovery skills: able to independently run interviews, analyze usage data, and translate insights into clear problem definitions. Comfortable operating independently within a defined scope, while aligning with broader product direction and company priorities. Strong technical aptitude and ability to partner effectively with engineers on infrastructure, data-heavy, or systems-oriented products. Experience defining KPIs and using data to guide prioritization and measure impact. Clear, concise communicator who can align cross-functional partners and maintain steady execution momentum. Demonstrated ability to make thoughtful decisions amid ambiguity, balancing customer value, technical feasibility, and business considerations. Datadog values people from all walks of life. title: Software Engineer, DevSec
description_clean: Ready to be pushed beyond what you think you’re capable of? At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. It’s a massive, ambitious opportunity that demands the best of us, every day, as we build the emerging onchain platform — and with it, the future global financial system. To achieve our mission, we’re seeking a very specific candidate. We want someone who is passionate about our mission and who believes in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to update the financial system. We want someone who is eager to leave their mark on the world, who relishes the pressure and privilege of working with high caliber colleagues, and who actively seeks feedback to keep leveling up. We want someone who will run towards, not away from, solving the company’s hardest problems. Our work culture is intense and isn’t for everyone. But if you want to build the future alongside others who excel in their disciplines and expect the same from you, there’s no better place to be. While many roles at Coinbase are remote-first, we are not remote-only. In-person participation is required throughout the year. Team and company-wide offsites are held multiple times annually to foster collaboration, connection, and alignment. Attendance is expected and fully supported. The Developer Security team secures Coinbase's software supply chain and builds automation to find and fix vulnerabilities at scale. We own artifact scanning, dependency analysis, vulnerability management, and security agents that automate remediation across the company. As a Software Engineer on this team, you will build and ship features across these systems. You will work with engineers across Coinbase to make security a natural part of how they build software. What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties): Build and maintain services to meet critical product and business needs using Golang. Deliver features and small-to-medium projects end-to-end: design, implementation, deployment, and monitoring. Author technical design documents and break down complex features into individual tasks. Write high quality, well tested code to meet the needs of your customers. Improve code quality and address technical debt within your ownership area. Participate in on-call rotations, code reviews, and incident response. Work with engineers, product managers, and security partners to deliver on the team's roadmap. What we look for in you (ie. job requirements): At least 2 years of experience in software engineering. You have built, deployed, and maintained production services and understand service-oriented architecture. You write high quality, well tested code. You can author technical design documents and break down features into discrete tasks. You actively use modern tooling (including AI-assisted development) to ship faster and learn new domains quickly. You are passionate about building an open financial system that brings the world together. Nice to haves: Experience with security tooling or security-adjacent engineering (e.g., SLSA, Sigstore, artifact scanning, SBOM). Familiarity with Artifactory, Xray, or similar supply chain security platforms. Experience with Golang, Docker, Kubernetes, Postgres, MongoDB, gRPC. Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS or GCP). You have worked at a company during a period of rapid growth. Job #: 75879

title: Senior Specialist, Industry Solutions
description_clean: To support our growth, we are strengthening our Solutions Assurance Team as part of the global Industry Solutions Team (IST), a flexible, globally distributed organization. Our mission is to position MongoDB as an optimized platform for AI‑enabled industry solutions and to deeply understand enterprise customers, their business context, and how they can modernize through artificial intelligence and data‑driven architectures. As a Senior Solutions Specialist, you will sit at the intersection of technology, integrations, and field enablement. You will own complex validation workstreams with partners, lead the technical strategy for key integrations with the MongoDB ecosystem, and translate deep technical findings into clear, outcome‑oriented guidance for internal and external stakeholders. This role is a great opportunity for experienced technologists who want to deepen their expertise in solution design, integration testing, and go‑to‑market enablement while working in a highly collaborative, cross‑functional environment at one of the fastest‑growing technology companies. Learn more about #LifeAtMongoDB on our careers site. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. What you’ll do at MongoDB In this role, you will: Own and lead complex solution validations across partner and customer technologies (for example: security tools, backup and recovery platforms, GenAI solutions, IoT/MQTT and streaming platforms), becoming a recognized subject‑matter expert in one or more domains over time Design, plan, and execute integration test strategies that ensure solutions are robust, performant, secure, and aligned with recommended MongoDB patterns, including clear definitions of scope, success criteria, and risks Produce high‑quality technical artefacts (integration guides, evaluation reports, test matrices, quick‑start notes, and architectural background papers) that are field‑ready and easily consumable by Solutions Architects, Sales, Product, and partners Act as a trusted technical counterpart to partners and internal stakeholders, explaining integration patterns, trade‑offs, limitations, and best practices in clear, non‑jargon language tailored to business and technical audiences (while not acting as product technical support) Partner closely with the wider Industry Solutions and Product teams to bring real‑world integration learnings into product roadmaps, documentation, partner programs, and early‑access initiatives Contribute to and improve internal processes and playbooks, bringing a data‑driven and process‑oriented mindset to how we plan, prioritize, execute, and report on validation work Mentor and support other team members, sharing best practices in testing, communication, and stakeholder management, and providing guidance on integration projects where you have subject‑matter expertise Represent Solutions Assurance in cross‑functional initiatives, including account strategies, partner programs, enablement sessions, and internal reviews, ensuring our perspective is reflected in key decisions Enjoy the ride as we continue to scale rapidly and expand our impact with partners and customers around the world What we’re looking for As an ideal candidate, you will have 5+ years of relevant experience in the technology sector (or equivalent technical experience), such as software engineering, solutions engineering, technical consulting, QA / integration testing, or similar roles where you have owned complex technical workstreams end‑to‑end Demonstrated ability and appetite to learn quickly and adapt to new industries, solution domains, and partner technologies, including emerging AI and data‑driven platforms Strong scripting and/or programming skills (e.g., Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, shell) with a solid grasp of modern application and operational architectures (cloud‑native, microservices, APIs, event‑driven systems, streaming) A proven blend of technical depth and communication skills, including experience presenting complex technical concepts clearly to architects, engineers, and business stakeholders Solid understanding of data, analytics, data engineering and/or data science concepts and how they are used to build AI‑enabled solutions; you are comfortable reasoning about data flows, data models, and performance implications Comfort and effectiveness context‑switching across multiple domains (for example, moving from backup and disaster recovery in one validation to GenAI search and retrieval or IoT streaming in another) Proven ability to manage multiple stakeholders and parallel workstreams, including setting expectations, flagging risks early, and driving decisions to closure A self‑starter mindset, with ownership, curiosity, and the drive to become an expert in your focus areas, including proactively identifying gaps and proposing improvements A strong team orientation, with a collaborative, process‑aware approach to achieving shared goals and improving the way the team operates As an ideal candidate, you may also have A Master’s degree in a technical or quantitative discipline, or equivalent experience in the tech sector Hands‑on experience with databases and/or deploying applications on major cloud providers or database platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP, MongoDB Atlas), including real‑world production workloads Experience with database programming and data modeling, including understanding trade‑offs in schema design for performance, scalability, and maintainability Experience in integration or functional testing using tools and frameworks such as Postman, Selenium, SoapUI, Cucumber, or similar Working knowledge of DevOps and CI/CD tooling, such as Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Bamboo, Docker, or equivalents, including integrating automated tests into pipelines Familiarity with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools like Terraform or Pulumi, particularly in the context of provisioning repeatable integration test environments A MongoDB certification or equivalent hands‑on experience with MongoDB products and the Atlas developer data platform Proficiency in more than one language, especially where it supports collaboration with our global customer and partner base What you will learn and gain In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Experience life inside a fast‑paced, high‑growth tech company and be part of a “can‑do” team where ownership and intellectual honesty are core values Deeply learn the rapidly expanding MongoDB product suite, including: MongoDB Atlas, our fully managed cloud database service Atlas Search and advanced indexing capabilities Atlas Vector Search, stream processing, and other AI‑enabling capabilities Numerous tools, services, and connectors in the MongoDB ecosystem Gain exposure to a wide range of industries and use cases, building a cross‑industry perspective on how organizations modernize their applications and data platforms with MongoDB and partner technologies Build practical skills in product and program management, including how we scope, prioritize, and execute integration and validation work across multiple teams Develop a deeper understanding of sales engagement models and qualification in a global company, and how technical teams (like Industry Solutions and Solutions Assurance) partner with go‑to‑market functions to drive customer and partner success Strengthen your ability to communicate complex technical findings in concise, field‑ready formats that influence decisions at customers, partners, and internally title: Manager, Tax Accounting and Provision
description_clean: Ready to be pushed beyond what you think you’re capable of? At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. It’s a massive, ambitious opportunity that demands the best of us, every day, as we build the emerging onchain platform — and with it, the future global financial system. To achieve our mission, we’re seeking a very specific candidate. We want someone who is passionate about our mission and who believes in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to update the financial system. We want someone who is eager to leave their mark on the world, who relishes the pressure and privilege of working with high caliber colleagues, and who actively seeks feedback to keep leveling up. We want someone who will run towards, not away from, solving the company’s hardest problems. Our work culture is intense and isn’t for everyone. But if you want to build the future alongside others who excel in their disciplines and expect the same from you, there’s no better place to be. While many roles at Coinbase are remote-first, we are not remote-only. In-person participation is required throughout the year. Team and company-wide offsites are held multiple times annually to foster collaboration, connection, and alignment. Attendance is expected and fully supported. Coinbase is on a mission to build an open financial system for the world, creating the infrastructure for a more innovative, accessible, and efficient economy powered by cryptocurrency. As a global company, we hold ourselves to the highest standards of regulatory compliance, security, and innovation while delivering on our vision to drive crypto adoption globally and empower economic freedom for billions of people. Role Overview: The Manager, Tax Accounting and Provision will lead our global quarterly and annual income tax provision process in accordance with ASC 740.  In this pivotal role, you will own the technical integrity of our financial tax reporting, managing SEC disclosures and providing the essential analysis to substantiate complex tax positions. You will act as a key liaison for external auditors, defending our technical judgments while ensuring alignment between tax compliance and financial reporting. Beyond the preparation of the global tax provision, you will champion operational excellence by building a world-class controllership environment and continuously automating processes to enhance efficiency and accuracy. What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):  Global Tax Provision & ASC 740 Technical Accounting Manage the end-to-end preparation and review of the worldwide quarterly and annual income tax provision in accordance with US GAAP (ASC 740). Drive the resolution of the company's most critical tax accounting matters. Provide the essential research and documentation needed to substantiate the Company’s tax position with respect to complex issues, specifically regarding non-recurring transactions, FIN 48 reserves, and realizability of deferred assets. Own the accuracy of the global Effective Tax Rate (ETR), deferred tax asset/liability balances, and current tax payable accounts. Financial Reporting & Audit Management Lead the preparation of income tax footnotes and disclosures for SEC filings (10-K and 10-Q), ensuring transparency and compliance with regulatory standards. Serve as the primary point of contact for external auditors regarding tax matters; manage quarterly and annual information requests, walk through complex calculations, and defend technical positions. Manage the return-to-provision process, reconciling filed tax returns against the Company’s prior period provisions to ensure accurate financial reporting and alignment between tax compliance and tax accounting. Tax Controllership & Process Optimization Drive the continuous improvement and automation of tax accounting processes to enhance efficiency and reduce closing cycles. Build and maintain a world-class tax controllership environment; ensure strict adherence to SOX controls, maintain robust documentation, and oversee tax balance sheet reconciliations. Collaborate with Finance, Treasury, and Accounting teams to ensure data used in the preparation of the tax provision is accurate. What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):  10+ years of relevant tax experience, ideally with a mix at both a Big 4 accounting firm and in industry. Significant experience with ASC 740 and managing global tax provisioning and controllership at a multinational organization, particularly e-commerce, tech, or fintech companies. Significant experience with the preparation and review of complex US consolidated federal income tax returns. Significant experience with US international tax concepts of Subpart F, foreign tax credits, GILTI, FDII and BEAT. Experience with One Source Tax Provision and Tax Compliance. Proven ability to lead, manage, and optimize direct tax processes. Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to effectively articulate complex tax concepts in a clear and concise manner to both technical and non-technical audiences, including cross-functional business teams. Highly motivated individual with a proactive approach and a commitment to excellence in contributing to team goals. Outstanding attention to detail and a strong aptitude for process optimization and execution. Nice to haves: Advanced degree in accounting and/or tax (MST, LLM). CPA, or CPA equivalent. Crypto industry experience. PID: P75546

title: Specialist, Industry Solutions
description_clean: MongoDB is the modern database platform and consistently ranked the #1 most wanted database by developers. We are trusted by some of the most demanding, innovative companies and development teams in enterprise software. To support this growth, we are expanding our Solutions Assurance Team as part of the global Industry Solutions Team (IST), a flexible, globally distributed organization. Our mission is to position MongoDB as an optimized platform for AI‑enabled industry solutions and to deeply understand enterprise customers, their business context, and how they can modernize through artificial intelligence and data‑driven architectures. As an Industry Specialist, you will be at the intersection of technology and business solutions. You will work hands‑on with partner and customer technologies, validate integrations with the MongoDB ecosystem, and translate complex technical concepts into clear, outcome‑driven guidance that resonates with industry stakeholders. This role is a great opportunity for technologists who want to deepen their expertise in solution design, integration testing, and go‑to‑market enablement while working in a highly collaborative, cross‑functional environment at one of the fastest‑growing technology companies. Learn more about #LifeAtMongoDB on our careers site. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. What you’ll do at MongoDB In this role, you will: Validate industry and AI solution integrations between partner software and the MongoDB ecosystem (for example: security tools, backup and recovery, GenAI platforms, IoT/MQTT and streaming solutions), becoming a subject‑matter expert in one or more domains over time Design and execute integration test plans to ensure solutions are robust, performant, secure, and aligned with recommended MongoDB patterns Document your findings and best practices through concise integration guides, technical notes, and background papers, ranging from step‑by‑step instructions with screenshots to deeper architectural overviews Collaborate with the wider Industry Solutions Team to support opportunities across startups and large enterprises, co‑creating solution architectures, industry‑specific demo scenarios, and collateral that accelerates customer success Enable the field and internal stakeholders by explaining integration patterns, constraints, and recommended configurations in clear, non‑jargon language tailored to customer and partner audiences (you will not act as product technical support) Partner with product and program teams by feeding back learnings from integration work, helping improve roadmaps, documentation, and partner programs in a fast‑moving environment. Contribute to internal processes and playbooks, bringing a process‑oriented mindset that improves how we plan, execute, and report on validation work Share knowledge and elevate the team, participating in peer reviews, brown‑bags, and informal mentoring as you grow in the role Enjoy the ride as we continue to scale rapidly and expand our impact with partners and customers around the world What we’re looking for As an ideal candidate, you will have 3+ years of relevant experience in the technology sector (or equivalent technical experience in other industries), such as software engineering, solutions engineering, technical consulting, QA / integration testing, or similar roles Demonstrated ability and appetite to learn quickly and adapt to new industries, solution domains, and partner technologies Scripting and/or programming skills (e.g., Python, JavaScript, shell) with a solid grasp of modern application and operational architectures (cloud‑native, microservices, APIs, event‑driven systems) A strong blend of technical depth and communication skills, including the ability to present complex ideas clearly to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders Foundational understanding of data, analytics, data engineering and/or data science concepts and how they are used to build AI‑enabled solutions (we’ll help you deepen this expertise on the job) Comfort context‑switching across multiple domains (for example, moving from backup and disaster recovery topics in one project to GenAI search and retrieval in another) Proven ability to manage multiple stakeholders and parallel workstreams, keeping projects organized and moving forward A self‑starter mindset, with ownership, curiosity, and the drive to become an expert in your focus areas A strong team orientation, with a collaborative, process‑aware approach to achieving shared goals As an ideal candidate, you may also have A Master’s degree in a technical or quantitative discipline, or equivalent experience in the tech sector Experience with databases and/or deploying applications on major cloud providers or database platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP, MongoDB Atlas) Hands‑on experience with database programming and data modeling for real‑world applications Experience in integration or functional testing using tools such as Postman, Selenium, SoapUI, Cucumber, or similar frameworks Working knowledge of DevOps and CI/CD tooling, such as Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Bamboo, Docker, or equivalents Familiarity with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools like Terraform or Pulumi A MongoDB certification or equivalent hands‑on experience with MongoDB products Proficiency in more than one language, especially if relevant to our global customer and partner base What you will learn and gain In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Experience life inside a fast‑paced, high‑growth tech company and be part of a “can‑do” team where ownership and intellectual honesty are core values Deeply learn the rapidly expanding MongoDB product suite, including: MongoDB Atlas, our fully managed cloud database service Atlas Search And many other tools, services, and connectors in the MongoDB ecosystem Gain exposure to a wide range of industries and use cases, building a cross‑industry perspective on how organizations modernize their applications and data platforms Build practical skills in product and program management, including how we scope, prioritize, and execute work across multiple teams Develop a deeper understanding of sales engagement models and qualification in a global company, and how technical teams partner with go‑to‑market functions to drive customer success Equal opportunity MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. About MongoDB MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. title: Product Manager II, Log Management
description_clean: About Datadog: We're on a mission to build the best platform in the world for engineers, operations, and security teams to understand, secure, and scale their systems, applications, and teams. We operate at high scale—trillions of data points per day, allowing for seamless collaboration and problem-solving among DevOps and Security teams globally for tens of thousands of companies. Our engineering culture values pragmatism, honesty, and simplicity to solve hard problems the right way.   The Team: Logs are one of the richest sources of operational, security, and business data: powering use cases such as troubleshooting, threat detection, high volume log optimization, business transaction monitoring, and compliance. We're looking for a Product Manager II to help build the next generation of AI-powered log management capabilities. Our vision is to make it radically faster and easier for engineers, operators, and security teams to ingest, explore, and extract actionable insights from petabytes of log data using AI-first experiences. You'll work across the log lifecycle, from ingestion and processing pipelines to exploration and analytics, partnering with engineering, design, and data science to ship features that leverage agentic AI, natural language querying, and intelligent automation to transform how customers interact with their logs. At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.   What You'll Do: Develop a deep understanding of the day-to-day life of infrastructure, operations, and security teams that rely on logs to measure the health, performance, and security posture of their systems. Identify and prioritize high-impact use cases where AI-driven log analytics can deliver outsized customer value,  such as intelligent log pattern detection, anomaly identification, AI-assisted root cause analysis, and natural language querying. Define and build experiences that combine Agentic AI, Workflows, and Analytics to create next-generation investigation and remediation runbooks for operations and security teams. Collaborate with engineering, design, and data science teams to build and iterate on AI-driven insights, automation, and workflow applications on top of log data. Drive customer adoption and engagement, working cross-functionally with GTM teams to bring these capabilities to market. Drive thought leadership by creating blog posts, webinars, and conference talks that showcase Datadog's AI-powered log management capabilities.   Who You Are: A Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or Engineering or equivalent experience. 3+ years of product management experience in AI/ML-powered product features (such as anomaly detection, NLP-based search, or agentic AI workflows), analytics, observability, or infrastructure. A proven track record as a technical product manager independently defining strategy and owning execution of a SaaS product or data platform. You are an AI-native product manager, you actively use AI coding tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) to prototype, analyze data, and accelerate your own productivity. You have a GitHub that demonstrates what you've built using these tools. Highly motivated to hit the ground running with a strong sense of ownership and independence.  Strong problem-solving, communication, and customer-centric mindset.   Bonus Points: Experience in Log Management, observability, or analytics platforms (e.g., Splunk, Elastic, ClickHouse, Tableau, Looker). Experience with databases, data warehouses, or SQL-based analytics at petabyte scale. Familiarity with major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) and concepts like cloud-native logging, log routing, and archival strategies. Datadog values people from all walks of life. We know not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. title: Product Manager II - Retail Tax, Transactions, and Performance
description_clean: Ready to be pushed beyond what you think you’re capable of? At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. It’s a massive, ambitious opportunity that demands the best of us, every day, as we build the emerging onchain platform — and with it, the future global financial system. To achieve our mission, we’re seeking a very specific candidate. We want someone who is passionate about our mission and who believes in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to update the financial system. We want someone who is eager to leave their mark on the world, who relishes the pressure and privilege of working with high caliber colleagues, and who actively seeks feedback to keep leveling up. We want someone who will run towards, not away from, solving the company’s hardest problems. Our work culture is intense and isn’t for everyone. But if you want to build the future alongside others who excel in their disciplines and expect the same from you, there’s no better place to be. While many roles at Coinbase are remote-first, we are not remote-only. In-person participation is required throughout the year. Team and company-wide offsites are held multiple times annually to foster collaboration, connection, and alignment. Attendance is expected and fully supported. Our Consumer Trade team is looking for a Product Manager to play a pivotal role in helping our users understand and manage their crypto taxes, portfolio performance, and realized / unrealized gains and losses across their Coinbase activity and beyond. You will lead the ideation, development, and execution of a best‑in‑class retail tax experience and tax center that simplifies an incredibly complex problem space—multiple wallets, on‑chain transfers, gas fees, and incomplete cost basis—while ensuring Coinbase can meet its tax reporting obligations in key markets. You will also own core parts of the consumer trading experience visited by millions of users every month, including transaction history and performance. You will leverage your understanding of financial transactions, tax concepts, and trading performance to build engaging, trustworthy experiences that help users manage their transactions, P&L, and tax obligations in one place. What you’ll be doing Own the Retail Tax, Transactions, and Performance roadmap : Understand user behaviors, emerging tax regulations, and market trends to conceptualize and implement a roadmap that makes it dramatically easier for customers to view their transactions, track their performance, and handle their crypto taxes (including handling use cases like multiple wallets, on‑chain transfers, and gas fees). Data‑driven decision making : Define success metrics and run experiments to evaluate the effectiveness of tax and performance experiences (e.g., completeness of tax profiles, reduction in missing cost basis, engagement with tax tools, downstream filing behavior). Use data and user feedback to continuously iterate and improve. Ship from 0→1 and 1→N : Own product development from conception to launch and beyond, iterating until you’ve created the most seamless, trusted retail tax and performance experience in crypto. Design for scale and seasonality : Ensure the Retail Tax and performance surfaces are reliable, performant, and intuitive during critical periods such as tax season and major market events. Align teams on a shared vision : Drive clarity and alignment across Consumer Trade and partner teams on the Retail Tax and performance vision, requirements, and execution plan, and keep everyone focused on the highest‑impact problems. What we look for in you 5+ years of product management experience, including time spent building or scaling consumer products, ideally in fintech, investing, tax, or other data‑heavy domains. Domain expertise in financial transactions and taxes, such as experience with tax reporting products, cost basis, gains/loss calculations, or evaluating trading performance. Strong analytical and data skills, with experience working on P&L, performance analytics, or similar financial reporting surfaces for retail users. Consumer UX experience and a strong product/design sense for presenting complex financial information in simple, understandable, and engaging ways. Experience building 0→1 products in high‑ambiguity spaces, creatively solving problems under constraints while maintaining a high quality bar. Ability to work with complex stakeholder sets, including Tax, Legal, Compliance, Finance, support, and external partners, and to balance regulatory, business, and user needs. Crypto literacy and hands‑on exposure to crypto products—ideally including on‑chain activity such as DEX trading or send/receive flows—so you can deeply understand the pain points of tax in a multi‑wallet, multi‑chain world. Excellent communication and influence skills, with a track record of shaping strategic decisions and driving alignment across senior stakeholders. An entrepreneurial mindset and ability to succeed in a fast‑paced, evolving environment. Aptitude for learning and the ability to quickly ramp on new and complex regulatory and technical domains. Nice to haves Direct experience with US tax reporting rules for digital assets (e.g., cost basis, 1099 reporting, wash sales, staking rewards).   Job# P75681 #LI-Remote

title: Senior Technical Services Engineer- Weekends

title: Datadog for Startups Engineering Lead
description_clean: The Datadog for Startups (DDFS) program helps the next generation of fast-scaling companies adopt best-in-class observability and security from day one. As the DDFS Engineering Lead, you’ll combine technical sales engineering expertise with ecosystem growth leadership to attract top startups into the program, showcase Datadog’s value early, and position Datadog as an essential platform for scaling. This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of startups, business development, technical evangelism, and software engineering—comfortable running a discovery call with a CTO one day, hosting a founder networking event the next, and even helping startups get Datadog instrumented quickly.   What You’ll Do: 1. Technical Sales Engineering & Forward-Deployed Engineering (FDE) project management Validate startup technical needs and current tooling during pre-program discovery. Deliver tailored demos that highlight Datadog’s differentiators, quick wins, and startup relevance (AI, infra scaling, security). Answer competitive engineering questions and position Datadog effectively against alternatives. Create urgency for adoption so startups realize value quickly within their credits period. Ensure a smooth technical onboarding experience by removing friction and showcasing “aha” moments. Help manage FDE engineering projects that are embedded directly with client sites, deeply understanding workflows, pain points, and business processes. Implement Datadog for clients, troubleshoot issues or connect clients to those in Datadog who can support. Translate technical details for business stakeholders and communicate customer needs back to product/engineering teams.   2. Ecosystem Growth & Awareness Represent Datadog for Startups at key events (accelerator demo days, hackathons, conferences). Host or co-host startup-focused webinars, dinners, workshops, and founder roundtables. Develop messaging, collateral, and thought leadership for founders and technical teams. Drive inbound interest and applications through community-building efforts. Work with the SF DDFS Lead to build and manage relationships with accelerators, VCs, and other ecosystem partners. Host office hours with select referral partner portfolio companies to actively communicate DDFS perks to their portfolios.   3. DDFS Program Improvements Help the DDFS team automate processes across the full lifecycle of startup sourcing to conversion across internal tools such as Salesforce, Marketo, etc.     Who You Are: Must-Haves 5–7 years in sales engineering, solutions architecture, technical BD, forward-deployed engineer, software engineering at a SaaS/cloud company. Strong understanding of modern startup tech stacks, including cloud infrastructure, application frameworks, data platforms, and developer tooling Proven experience engaging with startups, founders, VCs, technical decision-makers and audiences. Excellent communication skills: equally effective in technical deep-dives and business-level storytelling. Builder: eager to shape a hybrid BD + technical role in a fast-scaling program while also able to get hands on and build both for DDFS and with startups using Datadog. Storyteller: able to translate technical differentiators into startup-relevant business value. Startup advocate: passionate about helping early-stage companies scale successfully with Datadog.   Nice-to-Haves Strong knowledge of Datadog’s product suite and observability/security ecosystem. A former technical founder or engineering leader at a startup. Experience running or speaking at startup/technical events. Background in developer advocacy, community building, or technical marketing. Knowledge of VC/accelerator ecosystems and startup go-to-market motion. Hands-on ability to articulate Datadog’s differentiators against competitors (Grafana, Sentry, Clickhouse, New Relic, etc.) CRM proficiency (Salesforce, HubSpot) and ability to manage demand-generation workflows.   Why Join Us: Be at the forefront of Datadog’s engagement with the global startup ecosystem. Shape a high-impact program where technical expertise and community growth intersect. Help the next wave of unicorns adopt world-class observability, security, and performance tooling from day one.   Benefits and Growth: New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP) Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking An inclusive company culture, including Community Guilds (employee resource groups) Access to Inclusion Talks and internal panel discussions Free, global Spring Health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+ Competitive global benefits and giving programs   Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of employment and the nature of employment with Datadog.   #LI-Hybrid Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. The ADF system handles  hundreds of millions of queries per month, processing exabytes of customer data. About the Role This role is responsible for designing, building and testing high-impact projects and enhancing the scalability, performance, durability, availability and reliability of the Atlas Data Federation distributed systems. We value intellectual curiosity and honesty, and building together in an environment that prioritizes collaboration over competition. Our team is distributed across North America. The position is open to our “in office”, “flexible”, or fully remote working models with convenient work hours for each time-zone. If you are passionate about data management, distributed systems, and leadership, and are excited to work on high-impact, high-growth projects, we would love to hear from you. title: Product Counsel, Consumer
description_clean: Ready to be pushed beyond what you think you’re capable of? At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. It’s a massive, ambitious opportunity that demands the best of us, every day, as we build the emerging onchain platform — and with it, the future global financial system. To achieve our mission, we’re seeking a very specific candidate. We want someone who is passionate about our mission and who believes in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to update the financial system. We want someone who is eager to leave their mark on the world, who relishes the pressure and privilege of working with high caliber colleagues, and who actively seeks feedback to keep leveling up. We want someone who will run towards, not away from, solving the company’s hardest problems. Our work culture is intense and isn’t for everyone. But if you want to build the future alongside others who excel in their disciplines and expect the same from you, there’s no better place to be. While many roles at Coinbase are remote-first, we are not remote-only. In-person participation is required throughout the year. Team and company-wide offsites are held multiple times annually to foster collaboration, connection, and alignment. Attendance is expected and fully supported. Coinbase is leading the expansion of digital currency into the financial mainstream. We are building a world-class legal team to support Coinbase and its affiliates as we continue to grow the company in the U.S. and internationally. You will be working in a cutting edge and fast paced environment, where disruptive technologies and unconventional thinking are the norm. Given the nascent state of the law, novel legal guidance is needed! We are looking for a highly motivated, entrepreneurial, and experienced product counsel to join our product legal team as Counsel, Financial Services & Loyalty. You’ll be embedded with Coinbase product teams, and collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, UI/UX, and cross-department colleagues including privacy, regulatory and international counsel, Compliance and Finance. You’ll take a thought leadership role in advising our Product teams on specific aspects of product development and implementation. What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties): Join and contribute to a best-in-class crypto product legal team, embedded with the Financial Services & Loyalty team within our Consumer business. Integrate into the day-to-day operations of Coinbase Product, providing legal, business and strategic advice. Look around corners and creatively solve problems for the business as they plan, build, and execute on their product roadmap. Provide full product life-cycle counseling, from ideation and development to launch, operation and sunset. Ensure product compliance with applicable U.S. and international laws and regulations, in close collaboration with colleagues in Legal and the Compliance team. Identify issues that may fall outside your expertise, driving resolution by partnering with other members of the Coinbase legal team and with outside counsel. Work cross-departmentally, including with stakeholders such as Compliance, Communications, Finance, Tax, Security, Enterprise Risk Management, Data, Analytics, and Corporate Development. Respond promptly to a wide range of complex legal requests and coordinate closely with Legal department colleagues to ensure the swift delivery of smart, practical advice. Operate in a fast paced, demanding environment and pivot quickly to meet leadership's needs. Recognize priority initiatives and effectively manage your clients and your advice to scale your advice and provide the right level of guidance for request at hand. What we look for in you (ie. job requirements): Currently licensed in a U.S. state. J.D. or equivalent with 5+ years of legal experience, preferably with in-house experience with a consumer financial services business, a fintech or crypto/blockchain company. You have experience counseling product and engineering teams in connection with launching regulated financial / banking products and services in the fintech space, and have a clear understanding of the legal underpinnings supporting these products. title: TMS Compliance Lead Analyst
description_clean: Ready to be pushed beyond what you think you’re capable of? At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. It’s a massive, ambitious opportunity that demands the best of us, every day, as we build the emerging onchain platform — and with it, the future global financial system. To achieve our mission, we’re seeking a very specific candidate. We want someone who is passionate about our mission and who believes in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to update the financial system. We want someone who is eager to leave their mark on the world, who relishes the pressure and privilege of working with high caliber colleagues, and who actively seeks feedback to keep leveling up. We want someone who will run towards, not away from, solving the company’s hardest problems. Our work culture is intense and isn’t for everyone. But if you want to build the future alongside others who excel in their disciplines and expect the same from you, there’s no better place to be. While many roles at Coinbase are remote-first, we are not remote-only. In-person participation is required throughout the year. Team and company-wide offsites are held multiple times annually to foster collaboration, connection, and alignment. Attendance is expected and fully supported. What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties): Investigate AML and fraud-related alerting transaction on the blockchain; write and/or file Suspicious Activity Reports (SAR) Conduct investigative review and analysis in support of Coinbase AML regulatory compliance policies and programs including investigating automated alerts from our transaction monitoring systems, and referrals sourced from law enforcement requests and other Coinbase lines of business  Analyze transaction activity and KYC information and conduct due diligence research in support of investigations Document investigations in written narratives Decide whether activity warrants escalation for additional review and subsequently recommend whether activity appears suspicious and warrants filing of a Suspicious Activity Report Maintain detailed documentation to demonstrate compliance with regulations and internal policies and procedures Remain current with industry standards and developments in the areas of KYC, BSA/AML, and OFAC, and apply regulatory requirements and internal policies and procedures to case investigations What we look for in you (ie. job requirements): Strong interpersonal, analytical, and communication (verbal and written) skills Experience working with Google Sheet, Google Doc, Excel, Word Flexible and adaptable to the evolving needs of a high-growth and fast paced organization environment Ready to support in 24*7 environment Organized with a High level of attention to detail Nice to haves: Professional certification in AML or Fraud (e.g. CAMS, CFCS, CFE) Proficient in blockchain analysis (e.g. Chainalysis) Experience in project management, analytics, or vendor management Advanced degree in business, finance, or customer experience (CX) Proficiency with tools such as Google apps, JIRA, Hummingbird, and Salesforce Service Cloud.     P71365 #LI-Hybrid

title: Business Value Consultant
description_clean: Business Value Consulting (BVC) is a critical, client-facing role where the consultant will work closely with the MongoDB Sales team, playing a key role in driving the business forward. In a pre-sales perspective, BVCs engage with clients to capture and communicate the potential business value that our solutions provide. For post-Sales, BVCs validate the value, identify optimization opportunities and identify new growth opportunities. You will work across all industries, evaluating the business benefits and financial value to companies primarily in our Enterprise market.
